claim: Skills encapsulate known-path workflows into deterministic steps; agents without harnesses produce unreliable outputs
actionable: For repeatable workflows, create skills that scaffold agentic loops with explicit instructions rather than leaving agents to iterate.
source_title: Claude Skills VS Agents Finally Solved

claim: Harnesses (context + structure + capabilities) wrap LLMs to govern behavior; same model + different harness = different outputs
actionable: Design agent harnesses with guides, sensors, tools, memory, state, and file system for complete contextual governance.
source_title: Claude Skills VS Agents Finally Solved

claim: Anthropic's known-path rule: skills for deterministic paths, agents for unknowns, hybrid for mixed scenarios
actionable: Classify workflows: pure plumbing (Make/Zapier), known-path (skills), unknown-path (agents), mixed (skill scaffolding with agentic substeps).
source_title: Claude Skills VS Agents Finally Solved

claim: Sub-agents isolate work in separate context windows to preserve main session context and manage token usage in skill chains
actionable: Create sub-agents for specific tasks (research, writing) that run in isolated contexts and report back to main workflow.
source_title: Claude Skills VS Agents Finally Solved

claim: Do not overuse AI: use Make/Zapier for 100% reliable plumbing, reserve agents for reasoning and edge cases
actionable: Evaluate: is this task pure data routing? Use Make/Zapier. Does it need judgment? Use agents. Mixed? Use determinism + agentic edges.
source_title: Claude Skills VS Agents Finally Solved

claim: Hybrid skill scaffolding lets agents handle edge cases within predetermined workflows, improving reliability over pure agentic loops
actionable: Define known steps as deterministic skills, add agentic sub-steps for unpredictable edge cases (e.g., customer intent classification).
source_title: Claude Skills VS Agents Finally Solved
